intro: Alcea calvertii, also known as the calvert's hollyhock, is a perennial plant species in the mallow family (Malvaceae), native to the mountainous regions of central and eastern Asia. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine to treat various health issues, including inflammation, fever, and respiratory problems, due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Alcea calvertii include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and triterpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal effects. In traditional herbal medicine, Alcea calvertii is often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are commonly used to alleviate coughs, colds, and other respiratory conditions.
